title: carex halleriana subsp. rorulenta
intro: Carex halleriana subsp. rorulenta, also known as the blue sedge or blue oat grass, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of this plant include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Carex halleriana subsp. rorulenta can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to calm coughs and promote relaxation.
